translations.en: He said: 'O Hurr! Are you against us or with us?' Al-Hurr said: 'By Allah, O son of the Messenger of Allah! I was sent to fight you. I seek refuge in Allah from being resurrected from my grave with my forelock chained, my hands shackled to my neck, and my face thrown into the Fire. O son of the Messenger of Allah! Where are you going? Return to the sanctuary of your grandfather, for you will be killed.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) said [in verse]: 'I shall go forth — for there is no shame in death for a young man, if his intent is true and he strives as a Muslim. Who relieves the righteous men through his own soul, departs from the wretched and opposes the criminal. If I die, I will not regret; if I live, I will not be blamed. It is shame enough for you to die in humiliation and submission.' Then al-Husayn (peace be upon him) set out until he stopped at al-Qatqatana. He saw a pavilion pitched there and said: 'To whom does this pavilion belong?' It was said: 'To 'Ubaydullah b. al-Hurr al-Ju'fi.' So al-Husayn (peace be upon him) sent for him and said: 'O man! You are a sinner in error, and Allah will surely take you to account for what you do if you do not repent to Allah this very moment, and support me — that my grandfather may intercede for you before Allah.' He said: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah! By Allah, if I supported you, I would be the first to be killed before you. But here is my horse — take it.' Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) turned his face away from him and said: 'We have no need of you or your horse. I would not take the misguided as supporters. Leave, and do not be with us nor against us. Surely, whoever hears our cry — we the Ahl al-Bayt — and does not respond, Allah will throw him face-first into Hell.' Then he set out until he stopped in Karbala'. He said: 'What place is this?' It was said: 'This is Karbala', O son of the Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'By Allah, this is a day of distress and affliction (karb wa bala'). This is the place where our blood will be shed and our sacred inviolability violated.' Then 'Ubaydullah b. Ziyad came with his army and encamped at al-Nakhila. He sent 'Umar b. Sa'd to al-Husayn commanding four thousand horsemen.
